title: Using Axios and Advanced Patterns
description: Explore the Axios library for more features and request/response interceptors.

slides.title: Why Axios? 🤔
slides.content:
# A Popular Alternative

Axios is a promise‑based HTTP client that works in the browser and Node.js (and React Native). It offers:
- Automatic JSON transformation
- Interceptors (modify requests/responses globally)
- Cancellation
- Timeout handling
- Better error handling (HTTP errors reject the promise)

Installation:
```bash
npm install axios
```

slides.title: Axios GET Example
Axios POST
Interceptors

quizData.question: How does Axios handle HTTP error statuses (like 404) compared to fetch?

options.text: It resolves the promise, you must check status

quizData.explanation: Axios rejects the promise for any HTTP status outside 200‑299, making error handling simpler.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Create an Axios instance with default configuration (baseURL, headers) and export it. Then use that instance throughout your app.
